world.title: NATO's eastern members brace for US disengagement
world.description: US President Donald Trump has once again questioned the value of the transatlantic military alliance. Russia's neighbors in the Baltic region are watching his statements with concern.

world.title: US Iran ceasefire live updates
world.description: A fragile two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran showed further strain on Friday, a day before they are to negotiate in Pakistan, as Washington accused Tehran of breaching promises on the Strait of Hormuz and Israel struck Lebanon with attacks that Iran has claimed violate the truce

world.title: From critic to negotiator: JD Vance to spearhead US efforts in Iran ceasefire talks
world.description: Vance faces major test in Islamabad talks, a key moment for 2028 hopeful amid challenges ahead

world.title: Widespread Flooding In Angola Leaves Communities In Crisis
world.description: Flooding in Angola has inundated homes, leaving residents pleading for assistance as communities grapple with rising water levels and deteriorating living

world.title: Rhondda drug dealer busted after sending Turkey teeth picture
world.description: A police investigation led to Coran Davies being arrested for multiple offences, including drug dealing.

world.title: Eswatini Supreme Court allows lawyer access to men in US deportation case
world.description: The Supreme Court in Eswatini has ruled that four men deported by the U.S. under a third-country program can finally meet with a lawyer.

world.title: Global Leaders Convene at United Nations CSW70 to Advance Gender Justice Through Science-Based Solutions
world.description: New York, NY, United States, April 10, 2026 /MarketersMEDIA/ -- New York, March 18, 2026, At the 70th Session of the Commission on the Status of Women (CSW70), a distinguished gathering of global leaders, policymakers, and changemakers convened at the United Nations for a high-level side event addressing one of the most urgent imperatives of our time: dismantling structural barriers and advancing gender justice through science-based, inclusive solutions. The session was moderated by Prof. Ada Juni Okika, Senior Adviser to the Permanent Mission of the Pan African Intergovernmental Agency for Water and Sanitation for Africa to the United Nations. H.E. Ambassador Dr. Nabhit Kapur, Permanent Observer, delivered key institutional remarks that framed the urgency and global significance of the agenda, while Dr. Raul Handa, DBA, Special Envoy to the Permanent Observer at the United Nations, played a pivotal role in convening and bringing together the distinguished group of global speakers for this dialogue, reinforcing the collaborative spirit and international depth of the session.

world.title: Promoting Lifecycle Refrigerant Management and Simultaneous Action on Cooling Efficiency at China Refrigeration Expo 2026
world.description: Organized by UNEP OzonAction, the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), the Foreign Environmental Cooperation Center of the Ministry of Ecology and Environment of China (FECO-MEE), and the China Refrigeration and Air-Conditioning Industry Association (CRAA), the O2C initiative has, since 2011, supported knowledge sharing on alternative technologies through global and regional platforms. Now in its 15th edition in China, the Roadshow and Roundtable have become part of the implementation of Chinaâs HCFC Phase-out Management Plan for the refrigeration servicing sector, supported by the Multilateral Fund for the Implementation of the Montreal Protocol.

business.title: IBM: How robust AI governance protects enterprise margins
business.description: To protect enterprise margins, business leaders must invest in robust AI governance to securely manage AI infrastructure.

business.title: Ares Plans a Smaller Private Credit Fund With Less Leverage
business.description: Ares Management Corp. is planning a significantly smaller flagship US direct lending fund than its previous record-breaking vehicle of $33.6 billion to speed up the deployment of capital amid broader dislocations in private markets.

business.title: More Air Canada Cafes Open In Montreal & Vancouver, Bringing Network To Six
business.description: The Air Canada Cafe concept has been expanded to six locations, with new lounges in Montreal (YUL) and Vancouver (YVR).

business.title: A common nutrient could supercharge cancer treatment
business.description: A common eye-health nutrient, zeaxanthin, may also help the body fight cancer more effectively. Scientists discovered it strengthens T cells and enhances the impact of immunotherapy treatments. Found in everyday vegetables and supplements, it’s safe, accessible, and shows strong potential as a cancer therapy booster. Human trials are the next step.

business.title: Stocks Eye Best Week Since May on US-Iran Hopes: Markets Wrap
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- The first inflation snapshot of the war-fueled spike in energy costs matched expectations, driving stocks higher ahead of this weekend’s planned peace talks between the US and Iran.

business.title: Stocks mixed, oil steadies on guarded optimism for Iran ceasefire
business.description: Stock markets were mixed and oil prices steady Friday with investors cautiously optimistic about the US-Iran ceasefire holding, and as a US inflation report was not as bad as feared.

business.title: TTC CEO orders immediate suspension of work car fleet
business.description: The back‑to‑back disruptions have frustrated riders and renewed scrutiny of the TTC's overnight maintenance operations.

business.title: Rising costs for food, labor, credit card swipe fees threaten Texas restaurants
business.description: Half of all Texas restaurant operators failed to earn a profit last year, according to data tracked by the National Restaurant Association.

science.title: Integrated Photonic Collection for Trapped Ions
science.description: Researchers present a novel ion-trap chip integrating photonics for efficient fluorescence photon collection, enhancing quantum information processing.

science.title: How Are Marsquakes Located? Exploring Sensing on the Red Planet
science.description: Tracking marsquakes with a single seismometer, scientists uncover Mars's internal structure, informing us about its past and potential for future exploration.

science.title: All Eyes on the Skies as Artemis II Returns to Earth
science.description: The crew of the Artemis II is heading back home to Earth with splashdown in the Pacific scheduled for tonight....

science.title: Gravitational waves may be hidden in the light atoms emit
science.description: Scientists have proposed a surprising new way to detect gravitational waves—by observing how they change the light emitted by atoms. These waves can subtly shift photon frequencies in different directions, leaving behind a detectable signature. The effect doesn’t change how much light atoms emit, which is why it’s gone unnoticed until now. If confirmed, this approach could lead to ultra-compact detectors using cold-atom systems.

science.title: Gravitational waves may be hidden in the light atoms emit
science.description: Scientists have proposed a surprising new way to detect gravitational waves—by observing how they change the light emitted by atoms. These waves can subtly shift photon frequencies in different directions, leaving behind a detectable signature. science.title: These cheap solar cells work better because they’re flawed
science.description: Perovskite solar cells shouldn’t work as well as they do—but they do. Scientists have now discovered that defects inside the material actually help, creating networks that separate and guide electric charges efficiently. Using a novel imaging method, they revealed hidden structures acting like charge “highways.” This insight could unlock even more powerful, low-cost solar cells.

science.title: This superconductivity dies then comes back to life
science.description: A strange new kind of superconductivity has been uncovered in uranium ditelluride (UTe2), where electricity flows with zero resistance—but only under extremely strong magnetic fields that should normally destroy it. Even more surprising, the superconductivity disappears at first and then dramatically reappears at even higher fields, earning it the nickname the “Lazarus phase.”
